items.title: More uncounted votes discovered in SA a month after election

children.content: More uncounted ballots have been discovered a month on from the South Australian election, the state government says.

articles.title: Missing votes counted in key SA seat see One Nation margin increase

children.content: One Nation has retained the South Australian seat of Narungga, following a count of ballots that were missed on election day.

image.altText: One Nation's Chantelle Thomas at a media conference.

articles.title: Uncovered ballots prompt new count in key SA seat won by One Nation

children.content: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has uncovered more than 600 ballot papers that were not counted in the recent state election, including dozens from the closely contested seat of Narungga on the Yorke Peninsula.

image.altText: A composite image of two women with blonde hair

gallery.altText: A hand puts a lower house vote into a sealed ballot box as another person points to the top of the box

thumbnail.altText: A hand puts a lower house vote into a sealed ballot box as another person points to the top of the box

items.title: SA childcare centre forced to close over seven supervision incidents

children.content: Another Adelaide childcare centre has been forced to shut its doors for three months after seven incidents of children being left unsupervised.

image.altText: Edge Early Learning Centre Munno Para West

items.title: As flight options shrink, this regional city is hoping for a return to rail

children.content: Trains stopped coming to Mount Gambier decades ago, but with fewer flights, locals want them back.

image.altText: A man wearing a red and brown striped jumper stands underneath a black sign with white writing which reads "Mount Gambier"

items.title: 'It's just bulldozed through': Native title holders allege cultural sites have been destroyed by mining company

children.content: A First Nations group on South Australia's Yorke Peninsula says a mining company has bulldozed areas of cultural significance and has started development without a formal agreement with Traditional Owners, but the company says it has "continuously engaged" with the group.

gallery.altText: Two Aboriginal men in black shirts standing alongside each other with a serious expression to the camera

thumbnail.altText: Two Aboriginal men in black shirts standing alongside each other with a serious expression to the camera

items.title: AFL apologises after Butters appeal upheld, umpire abuse conviction quashed

children.content: The AFL appeals board has quashed Zak Butters's conviction for abusive language against umpire Nick Foot, saying a Tribunal member listening in while driving his car for part of the original hearing constitutes a miscarriage of justice.

image.altText: AFL star midfielder Zak Butters.

items.title: Labor's warning over 'timing' of Mount Barker, Murray Bridge commitments

children.content: The Malinauskas government made $300 million in election promises for the cities of Mount Barker and Murray Bridge, but when they will be delivered remains uncertain after the two cities did not elect Labor MPs.

image.altText: A woman wearing red and a man in a suit look at a red sign which reads: Labor's plan for Murray Bridge

items.title: Teen accused of toilet block assault took 'calculated steps', court hears

children.content: Prosecutors say the allegations against a teenage boy accused of sexually assaulting a child in a toilet block are so serious he should face trial in an adult court.

image.altText: A street sign that sayd "Regano Rd", with another reading "Toilet" below it.

items.title: Wine grape grower bakes cupcakes to pay bills as fuel costs bite in 'worst' harvest

children.content: Farmers in the midst of harvest in South Australia are having to get creative to help make ends meet.

image.altText: A fair-skinned woman in a red floral dress holds a cupcake on a glass plate in a kitchen.

items.title: Blood donation eligibility expands for gay and bisexual men, transgender people

children.content: From today, Australian Red Cross Lifeblood will use gender-neutral assessments for blood and platelets donations.

image.altText: a man sits in a reclinable chair with a bandage on his inner elbow and he squeezes a stress ball

items.title: Potato company fined $360,000 after propeller 'severely damaged' employee's arm

children.content: A Southern Mallee Potato company has been convicted and fined $360,000 after a plane propeller struck an employee's arm, leaving him with multiple injuries, including partial finger amputations.

image.altText: A crate of potato's on a farm.

items.title: Woolworths's 'Prices Dropped' promotion broke guidelines, court hears

children.content: The consumer watchdog has taken Woolworths to court, claiming shoppers weren't receiving genuine discounts on hundreds of items.

image.altText: Close up of the green Woolworths W logo on the outside of a building.

items.title: Festival ticketholders waiting six years for refund after cancelled shows

children.content: Ticketholders are still waiting for refunds and answers from The Drop music festival.

image.altText: Audience and lit up festival stage.

items.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate's renewed defamation claim names Australian YouTuber

children.content: An Adelaide YouTuber has been identified in a renewed defamation lawsuit self-described misogynist Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have lodged with a US court against some of their online critics.

image.altText: Andrew Tate wearing sunglasses walks with brother Tristan

items.title: Octopus species mysteriously disappears from Spencer Gulf

children.content: The once-abundant pale octopus has not been caught in lower Spencer Gulf for more than a year, and what happened to the species remains a mystery.

image.altText: An orange octopus on a blue fishing bin lip

items.title: Last chance for answers as police admit error in search for Nannup Four

children.content: The family of a mother and daughter who went missing, along with two others, in WA almost 20 years ago are pinning their hopes on a last-ditch effort to uncover the truth of what became of them.

gallery.altText: Two men, a woman and a child holding their hands in a prayer pose.

thumbnail.altText: Two men, a woman and a child holding their hands in a prayer pose.

items.title: Rebel Wilson asked PR firm for website to attack movie producer, court told

children.content: Australian actor Rebel Wilson asked her crisis communications firm to publish a website that accused a producer on her own film of being an "Indian Ghislaine Maxwell", a Sydney court hears.

image.altText: Rebel Wilson arrives at court

items.title: Rattenbury denies quitting over coalition talks, but they can't have helped

children.content: In announcing his retirement from ACT politics, Greens leader Shane Rattenbury says he is looking forward to finding out what his next career move could be.

image.altText: A man with short hair wearing a blazer stands outdoors looking serious.

items.title: Diesel and fertiliser are shooting up in price

children.content: There are two things crucial to modern farming - synthetic fertiliser and diesel.
So what happens when the cost of both increases massively, but the crops they're used to grow remains the same price?
That's precisely the dilemma facing Australian farmers as they prepare to plant their winter crops for this year.
While some have chosen to opt out of the season entirely, others say they are pushing ahead and facing the uncertainty.

image.altText: A person with a beard is holding a jar of small white beads and smiling slightly. The setting is a bright room.

gallery.altText: A person with a beard is holding a jar of small white beads and smiling slightly. The setting is a bright room.

thumbnail.altText: A person with a beard is holding a jar of small white beads and smiling slightly. The setting is a bright room.

items.title: Earthquake hits off coast of Timor

children.content: The epicentre was at a depth of 31 kilometres but there was no tsunami risk from the magnitude-6 quake, according to Indonesia's geophysics agency.

image.altText: Satellite map showing where an earthquake has hit near Timor-Leste

items.title: Gold Coast man awarded prestigious Medal for Gallantry for Vietnam service

children.content: Sergeant Garry Chad is awarded the Medal for Gallantry for his actions in Vietnam's Long Khahn Province in 1971 that saved several lives.

image.altText: An old man wearing a blue suit with several military decorations

items.title: Six ways your smartwatch is lying to you, according to science

children.content: Wearable fitness technologies like smartwatches are worn by millions of people daily. But they're not as accurate as you might think.

gallery.altText: A woman in a red T-shirt looks down at a watch next to swimming pool

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a red T-shirt looks down at a watch next to swimming pool

items.title: I quit TV for a month, but the trick was what I did with myself instead

children.content: It was partly an experiment in self-control and habit-breaking, but also a response to the unhappy realisation that watching shows had become my crutch to zone out.

items.title: Living in an apartment with your dog? Here are four things to consider

children.content: Providing opportunities for your dog to explore, giving them a safe and comfortable space to retreat to — as well as a good routine — are key to keeping your dog happy.

image.altText: small brown dog laying in blue furry bed

items.title: NRL overhauls training rules in landmark player safety move

children.content: The move to restrict training loads is aimed at mitigating injuries, including player head injuries, which continue to be a major issue for all contact sports.

gallery.altText: a rugby league coach at training

items.title: Man jailed for mimicking shooting on bridge used in Bondi attack

children.content: A man who yelled antisemitic profanities while pretending to shoot at people from a pedestrian bridge used by the Bondi Beach terror attack gunmen has been sentenced.

image.altText: Bondi Beach bridge three weeks after terrorist attack.

items.title: Bitter with Australia for deporting him, he hatched a plan to get even

children.content: Eneasi Taumoefolau's 2024 arrest on drugs charges ignited fears the notoriously violent outlaw Comancheros gang had infiltrated "the Friendly Islands".

image.altText: A man sits for an interview.

items.title: 'Outrageous mess': About 2,000 AI road safety camera fines withdrawn in WA

children.content: About 2,000 fines valued at $1.1 million issued as a result of AI-assisted road safety cameras in WA have been withdrawn, six months after the devices were introduced.

items.title: Woolworths fronts court on dodgy discount allegations, ASX slips — as it happened

children.content: Supermarket giant Woolworths faced off with the consumer watchdog in court on Tuesday. The ASX 200 slipped into the red. Catch up on the day's events on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Labor secretary leaves Trump's cabinet after abuse of power allegations

children.content: The Labor secretary's departure was announced by a White House aide on Monday, local time.

image.altText: Lori Chaves-DeRemer wearing a black blazer.

items.title: F1 makes 'sensible changes' after engine concerns to start season

children.content: The next Formula 1 race in Miami will be a testing and proving ground for a number of new rules aimed at improving performance and safety.
